surface: Private Milestones 3–4 source/test implementation: the first native element/prop/event/ref slice; Lynx-specific callback-demanded list cells with item-key identity, reuse pools, and physical ref attachment; root-scoped serializable native-resource handles; augmentable init/global/Native Module types and background platform hooks; and separate background/main-thread compiler presets. No production .lynx.bundle, native allocation, scrolling, lifecycle, Native Module, custom-element, or device claim is made.
divergences: The package adapts an audited @lynx-js/types@4.0.0 props/events slice into renderer-owned module declarations because the upstream events entry transitively loads global Element/JSX augmentation.
Background refs receive cloned acknowledgement-gated asynchronous handles, never live main-thread Element PAPI objects or synchronous layout.

A recycled list item remains a logical Octane subtree while its physical native cell is absent. Refs detach and reattach through the optional universal host-attachment capability; viewport recycling alone does not run component or effect cleanup.
Background event handlers receive detached data-only payloads without live event methods or native instances.
Main-thread receiver installation is explicit in this private source phase; automatic Rspeedy production bootstrap is deferred to Milestone 5.

Platform APIs are background-only; the main-thread compiler preset rejects @octanejs/lynx/platform imports and statically visible NativeModules access. createLynxRoot() separately verifies the public background-only lynx.getJSModule() surface at runtime. The reload export only requests lynx.reload(); it is not a framework reload receiver.
Nested lists, portals, Lynx Suspense proof, lazy bundles, gestures, animations, full boolean-defer parity, and defer.unmountRecycled semantics are excluded from the initial Milestone 4 surface.
ssr: Unsupported. Lynx first-screen adoption is a native runtime protocol, not HTML SSR or Octane hydration.
notes: Milestone 0 remains blocked on public lifecycle/event hooks, reload/background teardown, Web execution, and the Android/iOS engine matrix; this private implementation is not a technical preview.

Native __AddEvent token registration is public, but the production background callback receiver remains the private lynxCoreInject.tt.publishEvent hook; dispatchNativeEvent* is a source/test bridge only.

Milestone 3 implements the host boundary for class strings, inline styles, CSS IDs/scopes, and bundled URL strings. CSS import/CSS Module extraction, stylesheet/template sections, and asset bundling remain Milestone 5 work.

Octane submits a complete next dataset bag through __SetDataset. @lynx-js/testing-environment@0.3.0 implements that operation with Object.assign, so key deletion is adapter-call evidence only until a real-engine lane proves it.

The public types expose createSelectorQuery().select(string), while selectUniqueID is commented out. Octane therefore uses generation-scoped [octane-ref] selectors; the testing environment proves selection/setNativeProps only, so invoke/fields/path/measure, selector support, and layout results require real-engine evidence.

The official JavaScript environment models a 1,000-item logical list, callback-demanded materialization, native cell identity reuse, reorder, attachment ordering, and inert callbacks after teardown. It cannot establish Android/iOS native allocation, scrolling, layout, steady-state memory, or lifecycle behavior.

The deterministic Node-only allocation gate records 12 physical fake-PAPI cells for a 12-row visible window over 1,000 logical items, 988 reuses, zero remaining cells after teardown, and a 0.012 eager-allocation ratio. It is not a native memory or timing measurement.

Init-data source tests seed from public __presetData and consume the pinned framework-maintained current __initData snapshot to cover RESET key removal and the render-to-layout subscription race. Octane does not yet install or claim the native update receiver that maintains that snapshot.

Init-data and global-props updates, global listener replacement, Native Module typing, public reload requests, and error reporting have source/unit evidence only. Public native event delivery, page destroy, and framework reload/background teardown receivers remain blocked contracts.
The app-owned Android/iOS Native Module and custom-element files under examples/native-capabilities are illustrative, excluded from package files, and not compiled or device-verified.

Boolean list-item defer metadata is accepted and forwarded, but all initial Octane list cells are callback-demanded regardless of that prop; this is not full ReactLynx defer behavior. ReactLynx's defer={{ unmountRecycled: true }} lifecycle behavior is not implemented.
Nested <list> hosts are rejected during batch preparation in the initial contract rather than entering a partially supported recycling path.
Milestones 3–4 formal exits remain unmet until equivalent pinned ReactLynx fixtures and native capability samples are compared through public observations on Explorer, Android, and iOS.
Production testing and .lynx.bundle integration remain Milestone 5 work.
